# **THE PLANES**

Beyond the confines of the Universe lie the vast planes of existence referred to as the Great Beyond. Often alien and dangerous, most of these planes embody some foundational aspect of reality-one of the chief elements that make up the rest of the multiverse or a kind of fundamental energy. Each plane is a reality unto itself, with its own laws of existence and its own native inhabitants who might visit, grant benefits to residents of, or cause havoc on the Universe.

# | PLANAR TRAITS

Each plane, dimension, and demiplane has its own properties and attributes. Planar traits can be broken down into five categories: scope, gravity, time, morphic, and planar essence. Combined, those traits describe the laws and makeup of the plane. These appear in the plane's traits entry, though any trait that matches the Universe [described in the Normal entry in each section below) is omitted.

# SCOPE TRAIT

Most planes are immeasurable, so immense they're impossible to quantify. Which immeasurable planes, if any, are infinite is a subject of debate among philosophers and scholars alike. Since so many planes are immeasurable, those planes omit a scope trait. Otherwise, the plane likely has either the finite or unbounded trait.

**Finite:** Finite planes consist of a limited amount of space.

**Immeasurable:** Immeasurable planes are immeasurably large, perhaps infinite.

**Unbounded:** Unbounded planes loop back on themselves when a creature reaches the plane's <sup>1</sup>edge.<sup>n</sup>

# GRAVITY TRAITS

Many planes have unusual gravity.

**Normal:**Bodies of great mass are the centers of gravity, and objects fall toward those centers with a measured amount of force relative to the size of the body.

**High Gravity:** As in normal gravity, bodies of great mass act as centers of gravity, but the force relative to the size of the body is greater than in the Universe. The Bulk of all creatures and objects is doubled, meaning creatures acclimated to normal gravity can carry only half as much. Creatures used to normal gravity move at half Speed and can jump only half as high and far. Physical ranged attacks are impossible beyond the third range increment [instead of the sixth). Creatures that fall in high gravity take bludgeoning damage equal to the distance they fell.

**Low Gravity:** As in normal gravity, bodies of great mass act as centers of gravity, but the force relative to the size of the body is less than in the Universe. The Bulk of all creatures and objects is halved, meaning creatures acclimated to normal gravity can carry twice as much and jump twice as high and far. Physical ranged attacks are possible up to the

twelfth range increment [instead of the sixth). Creatures that fall in low gravity take no damage for the first 10 feet of a fall, and then take bludgeoning damage equal to a quarter of the remaining distance it fell.

**Microgravity: There's** little to no gravity on this plane. Creatures float in space unless they can push off a surface or use some force to propel themselves throughout the plane.

**Strange Gravity:** All bodies of mass are centers of gravity with roughly the same force. A creature can stand on any solid objects that's as large as or larger than themself.

**Subjective Gravity: All** bodies of mass **can** be centers of gravity with the same force, but only if a non-mindless creature wills it. Unattended items, objects, and mindless creatures treat the plane as having microgravity. Creatures on a plane with subjective gravity can move normally along a solid surface by imagining "down" near their feet. Designating this downward direction is a free action that has the concentrate trait. If suspended in midair, a creature can replicate flight by choosing a 'down'' direction and falling in that direction, moving up to their Speed or fly Speed. This pseudo-flight uses the Fly action.

# TIME TRAITS

Time flows differently on many planes.

**Normal:**Time passesthe sameway it does in the Universe. One hour on a plane with normal time equals1 hour in the Universe.

**Erratic:** Time slows down and speeds up, so an individual might lose or gain time as they move between planes. When a creature moves from a plane with erratic time to one with normal time, roll a DC 11 flat check. Creatures that leave an erratic time plane together share the same result.

**Success** Time passed normally on the erratic time plane.

**Failure** For each hour spent on the erratic time plane, 1 day passed on the normal time plane.

**Critical Failure** For each round spent on the erratic time plane, 1 day passed on the normal time plane.

**Flowing:** The flow of time is consistently faster or slower. A creature might travel to one of these planes, spend a year there, and find that only an hour passed in the Universe; alternatively, they might spend a minute on this plane and find out an hour passed in the Universe.

**Timeless:** Time still passes, but the effects of time are diminished. Creatures on these planes don't feel hunger, thirst,

or the effects of aging or natural healing. The effects of poison, diseases, and other kinds of healing might also be diminished on certain timeless planes. Spell energy and other effects still dissipate, so the durations of spells and other effects function as normal. The danger of this trait is that when a creature leaves a timeless plane and enters a plane with another time trait, the effects of hunger, thirst, aging, and other effects slowed or arrested by the timeless trait occur retroactively in the instant of transition, possibly causing the creature to immediately starve or die of old age.

# MORPHIC TRAITS

This trait describes how easily the physical nature of the plane can be changed. The Universe is considered the norm for its residents, but other planes can warp through the plane's own sentient designs or be manipulated by extremely powerful creatures.

**Normal:** Objects remain where they are [and what they are] unless affected by physical force or magic. Creatures can change the immediate environment as a result of tangible effort, such as by digging a hole.

**Metamorphic:** Things change by means other than physical force or magic. Sometimes spells have morphic effects. Other times, the plane's nature is under the control of a deity or power, or the plane simply changes at random.

**Sentient:** The plane changes based on its own whims.

**Static:** Visitors can't affect living residents of the plane or objects the denizens carry in any way. Any spells that would affect those on the plane have no effect unless the static trait is somehow removed or suppressed.

# PLANAR ESSENCE TRAITS

Planar essence traits describe a plane's fundamental nature. For example, many of the Inner Sphere's planes are infused with an element or energy, each of which affects magic on those planes, and the Netherworld is awash with shadow. Outer Planes are fundamentally made up of quintessence, a philosophically aligned material with infinite potential for shape and state that conforms to powerful and prevailing beliefs.

**Air:** Planes with this trait consist mostly of open spaces and air of various levels of turbulence, though they also contain rare islands of floating stone and other elements and energies. Air planes usually have breathable atmospheres, though they might include clouds of acidic or toxic gas. Earth creatures often find themselves at a disadvantage within air planes, as there's little solid ground for them to gain their bearings, which tends to at least make them uncomfortable.

**Earth:** These planes are mostly solid. Travelers arriving upon an earth plane risk suffocation if they don't reach a cavern or some other air pocket within the plane's solid matter. Creatures who can't burrow are entombed in the plane's substance and must attempt to dig their way toward an air pocket. Air creatures are ill at ease, as they rarely have the space to move freely through even the loftiest warrens.

**Fire:** Planes with this trait are composed of flames that continually burn with no fuel source. Fire planes are extremely

hostile to non-fire creatures. Unprotected wood, paper, cloth, and other flammable materials catch fire almost immediately, and creatures wearing unprotected flammable clothing catch fire, typically taking 1d6 persistent fire damage. Extraplanar creatures take moderate environmental fire damage at the end of each round (sometimes minor environmental damage in safer areas, or major or massive damage in even more fiery areas]. Ice creatures are extremely uncomfortable on a fire plane, assuming they don't outright melt in the heat.

**Metal:** These planes consist of chaotic and shifting structures and oceans of metal. Metal planes tend to exist in a state of change and decay, leaving plentiful pockets of air for visitors to breathe or move within. Creatures unlucky enough to be entombed in the plane's substance risk suffocation if they can't phase through metal. Wood creatures find the lack of stability and soil on a metal plane disconcerting and often fail to thrive in such environments.

**Water:** Planes with this trait are mostly liquid. Visitors who can't breathe water or reach an air pocket likely drown. The rules for aquatic combat (Royer Core 429) usually apply, including the inability to cast fire spells or use actions with the fire trait. Creatures with a weakness to water take damage equal to double their weakness at the end of each round.

**Wood:** These planes consist of trees and other flora latticed in organic patterns. Wood planes are often perfectly constructed to match their desired purpose or environment but aren't usually outright hostile to visitors.

**Shadow:** Planes with this trait are umbral with murky light. In the Netherworld, the radius of all light from light sources and the areas of light spells are halved.

**Void:** Planes with this trait are vast, empty reaches that suck the life from the living. They tend to be lonely, haunted planes, drained of color and filled with winds carrying the moans of the dead. At the end of each round, a living creature takes at least minor void environmental damage. In the strongest areas of a void plane, they could take moderate or even major void damage at the end of each round. This damage has the death trait, and if a living creature is reduced to 0 Hit Points by this void damage and killed, it crumbles into ash and can become a wraith or other undead creature.

**Vitality:** These planes are awash with life energy. Colors are brighter, fires are hotter, noises are louder, and sensations are more intense. At the end of each round, an undead creature takes at least minor vitality environmental damage. In the strongest areas of a vitality plane, they could take moderate or even major vitality damage instead. While this might seem safe for living creatures, vitality planes present a different danger. Living creatures regain an amount of HP each round equal to the environmental damage undead take in the same area. If this would bring the living creature above their maximum HP, any excess becomes temporary HP.Unlike normal, these temporary HP combine with each other, and they last until the creature leaves the plane. If a creature's temporary HP from a vitality plane ever exceeds its maximum HR it explodes in a burst of overloaded vitality energy, spreading across the area to birth new souls.

# **| PLANAR STAT BLOCKS**

Each of the planes listed in the following pages includes a short stat block of key information. The plane's type-whether it's a plane, dimension, or demiplane appears in the stat block's heading, followed by the traits that define that plane. The following entries also provide important information about each plane.

**Category:** This indicates whether the plane is an Inner Plane. Outer Plane, Transitive Plane, or dimension.

**Divinities:** A list of all of the deities, demigods, and other powers that call this realm their home.

**Native Inhabitants:** A sample of typical inhabitants of the plane. Also listed are the plane's shades, the souls of dead mortals who have been judged and sent on to whichever plane reflects the life they led.

# INNER SPHERE PLANES

The planes of the Inner Sphere form the heart of the cosmos. They're the home of mortal life, the focus of divine attention, the source of mortal souls, and the origin point of the great cycle of quintessence that fuels the motions and stability of reality itself. Arranged in a nested series of shells, like layers of an onion, the planes of the Inner Sphere include, from outer to inner: the elemental planes of fire, earth, metal, water, wood, and air: the mortal galaxies of the Universe; and at the very core of this cosmological ensemble, the raw forces of creation and destruction of Creation'sForge and the Void overlap the Universe.

### **THE UNIVERSE PLANE**

**Category** Inner Plane

**Divinities** The Newborn, Weydan

**Native Inhabitants** androids, barathus, humans, kasathas, lashuntas, pahtras, shirrens, skittermanders, vesk, ysoki, and countless other species \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The Universe is the prosaic realm and the home of mortal life. Innumerable galaxies contain countless stars and planets, each housing unique settings for any campaign, with the galaxy of Desna's Spiral as the classic example. The Pact System is the unofficial core of this galaxy, thanks to the presence of the Starstone, while countless other galaxies could be home to entirely different worlds, such as the distant solar system containing the planet Earth.

The Universe serves as the destination for pre-incarnate souls, each mortal life born, living, and dying before sending its spirit toward the planes of the Outer Sphere for judgment. The Universe is likewise the focus of the gods, each of whom is invested in fostering their particular portfolio among mortal worshippers on the countless worlds within.

# **CREATION'S FORGE PLANE**

**SUBJECTIVE GRAVITY | UNBOUNDED | VITALITY**

**Category** Inner Plane

**Divinities none**

**Native Inhabitants** jyotis, shades (enlightened) \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Creation's Forge is at once the source of life-sustaining vitality energy, the origin of all pre-incarnate mortal souls, and

paradoxically the most innately hostile of all of the planes. While vitality energy is deadly to undead and beneficial to living beings, such is the intensity of the plane that unmitigated exposure ultimately incinerates any extraplanar beings without sufficient magical protection. The most apt comparison for the plane's interior is that of the heart of a burning star. The stars of the Universe each house natural portals to Creations Forge within their glowing, potent cores to foster the movement of pre-incarnate souls in their first steps in the great cycle of life and death.

Brilliant and blinding, the plane's interior is sparsely populated, and the resident phoenix-kin jyotis view the Universe with active disdain. Some beachheads, such as the planar gateways to the Invemian System in the region of space known as Kazmurgs Absurdity, contain defensive garrisons of jyotis who guard their territory. A few scientific endeavors have utilized magitech innovations to travel into Creation's Forge and set up research outposts to try and answer the most pressing questions of creation and the origins of the soul.

**THE VOID PLANE**

**SUBJECTIVE GRAVITY | UNBOUNDED |**

**Category** Inner Plane

**Divinities** none

**Native Inhabitants** sceaduinars, wraiths, and other undead

The vast nothingnessof the Void is a merciless, lightless expanse of manifest destruction and nihilism. Sapping and consuming the life force of any living creature exposed to its energies, it corrodes and disintegrates material objects to rubble, then dust, and then nothing at all, yet the Void contains its own form of anti-life. At their densest concentration, the plane's energies aggregate into bizarre, black crystalline snowflake structures. These irregularities spontaneously generate the plane's resident sceaduinars. Dwelling in exquisitely lethal cities drifting in the vacuous darkness, these so-called void raptors are incapable of true creation and blame this flaw on some ancient betrayal by their rivals in Creation's Forge. Sceaduinars react violently not only toward creatures sustained by vitality energy, but also toward undead, whom they view as unnatural parasites unworthy of their plane's energies.

The deepest depths of the plane house the most resilient of undead creatures, which survive the constant entropy by virtue of their unliving state. Perhaps one of the most hazardous regions in the Great Beyond, the plane remains in a state of constant entropic advance that technology has yet to overcome. Some brave (or foolish] explorers make their way into the Void, whether to research or hide away from greater threats. Scores of Corpse Fleet agents fled here from the Universe, establishing great shipyards in the plane's most barren realms. The Degradation Swarm, a sentient swarm of self-replicating entropic nanites, meanders through the plane like a roiling thundercloud that converts everything in its path, with some of its tendrils being summoned into other planes of existence.

**| SUBJECTIVE GRAVITY Category** Inner Plane

# **ENHANCED AND IMPEDED MAGIC**

Some particularly potent areas can enhance magic related to the nature of the plane or impede magic inimical to it. For instance, a nexus of void energy in the Void might enhance void magic, and the unholy majesty of an archdeviI's throne room might impede holy magic.

**Enhanced Magic:** Anyone Casting a Spell with the enhanced trait gains a +1 circumstance bonus to their spell DC or spell attack roll with that spell.

**Impeded Magic:** A character who Casts a Spell or activates an item with the specified trait must succeed at a DC 6 flat check or the spell or activation is disrupted.

**Divinities** elemental lords of air

**Native Inhabitants** air elementals, cloud dragons, jaathooms, shades (air pneuma)

The Plane of Air, innermost of the Elemental Planes, is a vast realm of wind, storms, and skies. Illuminated by great artificial globes of flame and distant starlight from the material universe, the plane is populated by air elementals, dragons, air scamps, and an empire of jaathoom genies. Though mostly clouds and empty skies, the plane isn't entirely bereft of solid ground, including rock and ice created by the residents or dragged into their realm from the distant Plane of Earth or neighboring Plane of Water. The regrown pockets of the Plane of Wood have let loose giant pieces of driftwood into the plane as well. Bizarre, drifting spheres of brass and iron make for the most convenient navigational aids. The brass spheres have become known for housing the magitech metropolises of the vast Jaathoom Empire. The iron spheres were once shunned but have since become the site of constant extraplanar activity as corporations from the Universe establish Axis-reviewed contracts to mine these lucrative sites. Such mining endeavors have led to some material corporations abandoning their primary operations in the Universe and switching their focus to the Plane of Air.

The jaathooms rule from their shining capital city of Armun Kelisk, built atop a series of 10 floating islands. Stable planar gateways grant the Jaathoom Empire access to the skies of countless planes, but the pre-Gap innovation of the elemental engine once allowed starships to traverse the Plane of Air to circumvent long interstellar travel times. The advent of Drift engines challenged jaathoom dominance in interstellar trade, and some ruthless jaathoom merchant lords have since sought means of destabilizing Drift reliability.

For the past few centuries, the Plane of Air has been a political free-for-all. Jaathoom factions grab for power and influence while cloud dragon diplomats and barathu entrepreneurs seek footholds in the chaotic sky cities. Little is known of the plane's two reigning elemental lords: Hshurha the Duchess of All Winds and Ranginori, the Duke of Thunder. None have heard from the duchess since the end of the Gap, and even some of her attendants believe she's exiled or imprisoned, though she still grants spells to her supporters. Conversely, Ranginori has **PLANE OF AIR PLANE**

**PLANE OF EARTH PLANE**

IWI

**Category** Inner Plane

**Divinities** elemental lords of earth

**Native Inhabitants** crystal dragons, earth elementals, jabalis, shades [earth pneuma] \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

A great and rocky shell situated between the Plane of Fire and Plane of Water, the Eternal Delve hosts a unique and varied ecology of creatures at home in its rocky depths. Far from an endless, solid expanse, the Plane of Earth is riddled with great caverns and cave systems, excavated artificial vaults, vast crystalline geodes, and underground oceans and springs of magma where it borders its neighboring planes. Housing untold riches in gemstones and veins of precious metals, the Plane of Earth is an attractive setting for planar travelers seeking wealth and willing to risk danger and the wrath of elementals, jabalis, and other inhabitants who resent the plunder of their home.

Along with the Plane of Metal, the Plane of Earth has seen the most active interest from groups within the Universe seeking to exploit the plane for its abundant resources. Corporate- and government-sponsored planar strike teams routinely cross dimensional boundaries to execute tightly timed extraction and mining operations.Such actions rankle the hospitality of the jabali genies and their Peerless Empire, resulting in an ever-changing litany of treaties and sanctions between the Peerless Empire and representatives of a few powerful mining conglomerates from the Universe, including several dwarven clan corporations.

**PLANE OF FIRE PLANE**

laFl

**Category** Inner Plane

**Divinities** elemental lords of fire

**Native Inhabitants** fire elementals, ifrits. magma dragons, munsahirs, shades [fire pneuma]\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Like a great, gleaming ball of flame situated at the heart of the Astral Plane, the Plane of Fire is the outermost plane of the Inner Sphere. A perpetual ocean of fire with skies of smoke, storms of raining cinders, and lakes and rivers of magma flowing along its border with the Elemental Plane of Earth, the plane is incredibly hostile to those unprepared for its natural hazards.

This inhospitable realm is known most for one of the most visited metropolises in all cosmology: Medina Mudii'a, the bustling magitech hub of the Dominion of Flame. A spectacle of the tyrannical ifrits and their subjugation of much of the plane, Medina Mudii'a houses their vast armies and is home to the current grand sultana, **Ayasellah Mihelar Khalidlah VI** (inspiring female ifrit sultana]. The sultana has a less aggressive hand than her predecessors, and she rules with an appointed committee of advisors. Speculation is rife about the Dominion of Flame's smoldering ambitions, which seem to have coincided with the sudden disappearance of the elemental lord, Ymeri the Queen of the Inferno. Ymeri's disappearance is linked to the detonation of her former home, the Auroric Palace, which has since become the Auroric Crater. *■■■■■*

**PLANE OF METAL PLANE**

ICT1

**Category** Inner Plane

**Divinities elemental lords of metal**

**Native Inhabitants** metal elementals, shades (metal pneuma), taloses, zuhras \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The most sought-after plane by foreign interests in the modern technological age,the Plane of Metal is a cosmological juggernaut. Consisting of metals of every kind, including liquids and solids, the Plane of Metal is an expanse of cityscapes and oppressive magnetic fields. Even composite metals and swarms of rust flow freely throughout this plane, and the realms chaotic nature seems only tamed by the ever-increasing presence of magitech settlements. Scattered zuhra genies have suffered greatly from extraplanar exploitation and seek to rebuild their plane.

Laudinmio, the Sovereign of Alchemy, and Ferrumnestra, the Lady of Rust, are the two elemental lords of the plane. While other elemental lords often squabble or outright battle one another, the Plane of Metal's protectors have finally enacted a stable peace, allowing them and their servants to protect the plane from those seeking to harvest metals without regard for the ecological impact.

### **PLANE OF WATER PLANE**

**SUBJECTIVE GRAVHY[**

**Category** Inner Plane

**Divinities** elemental lords of water

**Native Inhabitants** brine dragons, faydhaans, shades (water pneuma], water elementals \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Beyond the skies of the Plane of Air, the roots of the Plane of Wood are nurtured by the vast, spherical, liquid shell of the Plane of Water. Its nearly limitless stretches of saline, fresh, and brackish seas teem with all manner of oceanic life, lit by submerged suns and bioluminescence before descending into the black, benthic depths where the plane borders the Plane of Earth. While it's perfectly amenable to water-breathing creatures, air-breathing travelers must provide their own magical or technological means to breathe while on the plane. Pressurized magic and metal moving cities trawl the varied depths of the plane, constructed and operated by faydhaan genies who have begun the process of reclaiming their once discarded empire. These settlements provide harbor for those seeking to escape other planes or those visiting briefly for trade or other business.

The previously numerous empires of merfolk have since been obliterated, with the significant majority of the species' survivors now taking on the solemn duty of guarding the imprisoned elemental lord, Kelizandri the Brackish Emperor. Sometime during the Gap, Kelizandri was overthrown and impaled by four vast magitech spears that pin him to the border of the Plane of Earth. Since then, Lysianassa, Empress of the Torrent, has come to prominence and helped usher the once-despondent faydhaans into a new golden age of trade and planar supremacy.

Unlike other subjective gravity planes, on the Plane of Water, a creature moves based on its swim Speed and must use actions to Swim if it doesn't have one.

**suBjEcnvEGRAviryJ**

**Category** Inner Plane

**Divinities** elemental lords of wood

**Native Inhabitants** ardandes, forest dragons, kizidhars, kodama, shades (wood pneuma], wood elemental \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The Plane of Wood exists between the Plane of Water and Air. The plane had a great resurgence following its return from seclusion in ages past but has since fallen into a state of rampancy. Like a fractal tree growth, it has seen less and less tending and shaping and has instead begun devolving into regions of asymmetry. The genies of the plane, kizidhars, once tended to the planes growth as artists but now do so more out of duress and necessity than for enjoyment.

Thanks to the technological evolution provided to countless civilizations by Triune's Signal, the importance of the Plane of Wood has greatly diminished. Civilizations in the Universe seek to build technological feats, focusing their attention on the resources from the Planes of Earth and Metal. Even factions like the Xenowardens can't provide support to the Plane of Wood as they focus their efforts on the rampant destruction of ecologies in the Universe. The two elemental lords of the plane have had a former disagreement escalate into all-out conflict. Shumunue, the Carved Lady of Mimicry, has sought to guide the plane's inhabitants to focus their efforts on tending the plane's rampant growth. Meanwhile, Verilorn, Custodian of Oak and Ash, radicalizes many to try to once again cut off the Plane of Wood from the other elemental realms in an effort to protect themselves and stem the ongoing threatening growth.

# TRANSITIVE PLANES

At a minimum, each Transitive Plane coexists with one or more other planes, a relationship oversimplified by stating that Transitive Planes are just used to get from one plane to another. The Drift has mysterious one-way connections to every plane, though those same planes can't access the Drift except through the same technology necessary by those in the Universe. The mists of the Ethereal Plane overlap the planes of the Inner Sphere, while the Astral Plane borders every other plane in existence like the backstage of the cosmos. Bright and dark mirrors of the Universe, the First World and the Netherworld overlap the mortal world, albeit often in bizarre ways such that a short distance in one might be a vast gulf in the other. The daring, wise, or desperate can utilize these planes to bypass barriers in the Universe or rapidly cross vast distances through much swifter travel.

**ASTRAL PLANE PLANE**

**SUBJECTIVE GRAVITY | TIMELESS**

**Category** Transitive Plane

**Divinities** Besmara

**Native Inhabitants** shades [untethered], shining children

Known as the Silver Sea, the Astral Plane surrounds the other planes of the Inner Sphere, separating them from the Outer Sphere. The River of Souls flows into this realm from the Universe, and souls move on from here toward the Boneyard for their final judgment.

**PLANE OF WOOD PLANE** As magic and technology fuse, more expeditions and probes into the Astral Plane have led to theologians and metaphysicists in the Universe observing the plane and its properties directly. Such explorers and scientists often find themselves threatened by soul-hunting daemons and other predators seeking their preferred food source-souls. The beings known as psychopomps lead a cross-section of nearly every type of celestial and monitor in existence, along with some fiends, to defend the flow of souls from predators and scavengers. Increasingly, this coalition of defenders also operates to keep the ever-increasing flow of "nosy mortals<sup>n</sup> out of their affairs and prevent them from affecting the ordained flow of the Universe's cosmology.

> Travelers within the Astral sea find the plane untouched by the passage of time, a property exploited by many mortals fearing old age. Time, however, isn't easily escaped, and upon exiting the Astral Plane, a creature finds this debt catching up to them, potentially aging to dust in moments.

**THE DRIFT PLANE**

**METAMORPHIC | SUBJECTIVE GRAVITY**

**Category Transitive Plane**

**Divinities** Triune

**Native Inhabitants** spectra

A sea of shifting colors, the Drift is the newest cosmological plane to appear. It first appeared 300 years ago when the tripartite deity, Triune, sent the Signal to all mortal species in the Universe. The Signal contained instructions for the creation of Drift engines that allowed technologically-advanced species to enter the new hyperspace plane and cross long distances at a much faster rate than using conventional space travel. With this advent, interstellar travel changed forever as countless species developed countless more engines, using the Drift to achieve faster-than-light travel previously tied to esoteric [or highly proprietary] equipment.

As only the technology of Drift engines can achieve entry into the Drift, other planes of existence remain relatively cut off from the plane, with one notable exception. In rare cases, when a Drift engine is activated, it somehow pulls a chunk of extraplanar matter from a seemingly random plane into the Drift with it. Matter pulled into the plane this way often emerges well beyond the scanners of a vessel, and most ships could never uncover the results of their engine's use. Still, it has led to a slow and steady buildup of extraplanar enclaves and holdouts across the emptiness of the Drift. While traveling in a starship to a destination, it isn't unheard of to encounter a group of celestials forced into piracy or a fiend-sponsored trading port.

The only native inhabitants of the Drift are the mysterious spectra and the deity, Triune. Spectra are unique beings created by the attraction of planar matter into the Drift. They defend technology and possess innate abilities to travel between the Drift and the Universe as though they had a non-magical internal Drift engine. The motivations of these beings remain as obscured as the history of the plane, though many point to the deity Triune and her immense metropolis of Alluvion that floats within the Drift.

**SUBJECTIVE GRAVITY**

**Category** Transitive Plane

**Divinities Alazhra**

**Native Inhabitants** hags, shades (terrorized), twinsouls \_\_\_\_\_\_

The Ethereal Plane, or Space Between Spaces, is a vast, misty realm overlapping each of the Inner Planes. Formed by the interactingtidal forces of creation and destruction from Creation's Forge and the Void, this plane swirls with currents and eddies of fog, lit only by erratic pulses of soft green luminescence and dim light from the planes it overlays, visible but ever intangible. Mortals once primarily used this plane as a means of shortdistance transit, whether it be bypassing barriers or escaping through an unseen realm. Thought to be a realm of ghosts and spirits, the Ethereal Plane is also home to unknowable dangers that dwell within the realm's protomatter mists.

More technologically advanced inhabitants of the Universe use the Space Between Spaces as a site for esoteric media creation. So-called "ghost hunters" use magical spells or equipment to traverse the border between the Universe and Astral Plane to confront spirits or haunting forces tied to this realm, often recording their endeavors for use as media upon their return. The predators of the Ethereal Plane haven't taken kindly to the monetization of their space, and many have begun following intruders back to their realms of origin by developing plane-splitting abilities.

A portion of the galaxy of Desna's Spiral is home to a planar convergence with the Ethereal Plane known as Pharasma's Coil. The phenomenon appears like a second spiral expanding within the galaxy, but visible only to those looking for the metaphysical signs of the Space Between Spaces. Worlds within this section have thin barriers with the Ethereal Plane, allowing ease of entry between the planes through specialized magitech devices closely guarded by the Church of Pharasma.

**ERRATIC | METAMORPHIC**

**Category** Transitive Plane

**Divinities** the Eldest

**Native Inhabitants** fey,linnorms \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The First World was a first draft of the Universe, crafted by divinities to test their metaphorical materials. A realm of extremes-bestial, primal, and beautiful—with colors and sensations brighter and more intense than the mundane world created after it, the First World is populated by fey and the divine entities known as the Eldest. Mirroring mercurial fey whimsy, the First World's laws of nature constantly and unpredictably change. Distance and time are wildly inconsistent, such that mortal travelers might spend an hour or a day within the First World, only to find a century or only a few seconds passed once they return to their own plane.

While many would consider the First World a primal paradise, this plane has seen a technological renaissance akin to those of the Pact Worlds since well into or before the Gap. Where once fey acted as proverbial (sometimes literal) "bogeymen" entering the Universe to whisk away mortals, they now take advantage of their plane's many gates to the mortal realm to abscond with

**ETHEREAL PLANE PLANE** new technology or adopt new fashion and media trends to bring back with them.

# **THE NETHERWORLD PLANE**

**SHADOW**

**Category** Transitive Plane

**Divinities** Lao Shu Po, velstrac demagogues, Zon-Shelyn

**Native Inhabitants** calignis, d'ziriaks, ixcaliads, kayals (fetchlings), shades [the mutilated), velstracs\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

A murky, distorted, and imperfect mirror of the Universe, the Netherworld overlaps the Universe and serves as a buffer or conduit between it and the Void. The Netherworld exists in a state of perpetually dim half-light, the landscape containing similar features to the overlapping Universe, but in warped or twisted fashions. Settlements in the Universe might exist in the Netherworld, sometimes in ruins and sometimes as terrible, frightening replicas. This applies to whole planets, though some worlds simply lack a counterpart in the Netherworld beyond a barren and lifeless rock.

Sometime during the Gap, it's known that servants of Zon-Kuthon operated interstellar ships using shadow drives, a type of engine that allowed travel into the Netherworld in a means similar to how Drift engines operate with the Drift. Such engines remain in use today, though the plane sees much less traffic with Drift travel being less horrific on travelers.

The shadow natives known as kayals are humans who have adapted to the Netherworld's cruel environment over countless generations. The fiendish velstracs long ago fled Hell and established themselves in the Netherworld. They've had a crisis of faith following the amalgamation of Shelyn and Zon-Kuthon into Zon-Shelyn, and they hope to solve their quandary of whom to serve through greater mutilations upon hapless flesh.

# OUTER SPHERE PLANES

**FIRST WORLD PLANE** The planes of the Outer Sphere are the manifest realms of philosophy: good and evil, order and change, faith, and their admixtures, populated by celestials, fiends, monitors, and others who promote these moral concepts. These planes are the backdrop upon which the mortal afterlife reaches its apparent conclusion, and the end destination of the River of Souls. The Outer Planes are regions of stability adrift in the raw, chaotic quintessence of the primordial Maelstrom, its tides forever gnawing at their edges even as mortal souls sustain them. The Outer Rifts manifest as cracks in the Outer Sphere's fabric. Rising from the metropolitan Axis is the Boneyard's spire, the location where mortal souls are judged and then sent to their final destinations, be they reward, suffering, or oblivion. The Outer Planes are places of majesty, wonder, terror, and danger outstripping anything mortal adventurers might encounter anywhere else.

**ABADDON PLANE**

**Category** Outer Plane

**Divinities** daemon harbingers, Horsemen of the Apocalypse, Urgathoa

**Native Inhabitants** daemons, shades (the hunted)

The wastelands of Abaddon are a nightmare vista of toxic fog **BONEYARD PLANE** and half-light where desperate shades attempt to hide from the depredations of the daemons who seek to obliterate their souls. Abaddon's perpetual eclipse might be nothing less than the lidded, comatose eye of the Bound Prince, the First Horseman, betrayed and cast down by the Four, forgotten by the cosmos at large, but far too powerful for them to destroy-waiting, watching, and hungering.

As the physical embodiment of death, the daemons exist in a state of constant predatory conflict only granting their service to the plane's leaders, the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse: Death, Famine, Pestilence, and War Settlements are rare and far between, with most specializing in the soul trade or the sale of items designed for differing types of murder, ranging from single targets to mass extinction events.

Of all the deific powers to change during the events of the Gap. few were as affected as the ranks of the daemonic harbingers. These demigod daemons typically swear fealty to one of the Horsemen, but some remain independent and try to establish broad power bases long enough to seize power. Rising harbingers, like Firjadah the Twisted Screen Name or Batwahr the Creeping Fallout, establish themselves as personifications of death related to modern innovations. Of the older harbingers, the Prince of the Wracked, Roqorolos,once served Apollyon but has since disappeared and turned his attention to the mortal Universe. This harbinger of fouled water has brought with him the rivers of Styx, which he's used to establish a foothold in a region of space in the Vast.

The Pallid Princess, Urgathoa, maintains her divine domain within Abaddon. Here, she protects the shades who seek their afterlife in her service while also courting powerful undead who seek her direct counsel.

**AXIS PLANE**

Category Outer Plane

Divinities Abadar, Lissala, primal monitors Native Inhabitants aeons, shades [remade] \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Axis is a realm of pure, absolute order, unhindered by moral concerns. The plane takes the form of a vast, gleaming, perfectly structured city. Built at the base of Pharasma's Spire, the Perfect City is a bulwark against the chaos of the Maelstrom and the Outer Rifts, with vast mechanical armies marching forth to explore, define, and pacify an imperfect, unruly universe. Axis is also home to axiomites: beings composed of living mathematics and equations. Axiomites continue to work on their organization of the cosmos but increasingly turn their attentions to the planar city of Axis itself: as with any city, Axis requires nonstop maintenance and improvement in order to resist the march of time.

Well-defined laws have ensured Axis is a hub for travelers from across the cosmos. One of the largest branch sites for AbadarCorp rests near the actual domain of the deity Abadar. The domain of Lissala has expanded to create a Rune Sector, a portion of Axis that acts as Lissala's domain and a meeting point for celestials, fiends, and countless other planar inhabitants looking to negotiate terms with a supposedly neutral arbiter.

**TIMELESS**

Category Outer Plane **INTRODUCTION**

Divinities Groetus, Pharasma, psychopomp ushers

Native Inhabitants psychopomps, shades [the dead)\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The Boneyard spans an impossibly tall and ever-growing spire of gleaming quintessence that rises up into the silver void of the Astral Plane. As the destination of the River of Souls, the Boneyard is where the souls of the mortal dead arrive for judgment and for Pharasma and her psychopomp servitors to direct them to their respective afterlives. Pharasma's domain is separated into eight courts, each corresponding to one of the other planes of the Outer Sphere and collecting the souls due to that plane. Not every soul goes unchallenged, and proxies of gods and planes argue and debate over souls, with final arbitration conducted by Pharasma herself.

Demigod psychopomp ushers serve Pharasma, many expediting the passing of mortals affiliated with specific planets. Conflicts in the Universe exacerbate the Boneyard's structure, with Arthalan First of the Last shepherding the inhabitants of the Azlanti Star Empire, while VrasiIon Wars End acts as the usher for loyal servants of the Veskarium. Both psychopomp ushers maintain cordial relations despite the workload their mortal charges impart upon them with the burgeoning war.

The great moon-deity of Groetus looms above the Boneyard, a herald of the end of times. His orbit has decayed much since the Gap, with the moon nearing completion of an apocalyptic collision with Pharasma's Spire itself.

Pharasma's courts and the surrounding Graveyard of Souls aren't the only features atop the spire. Beyond the graveyard, the Spirelands manifest environments much like those of the varied worlds of the Universe, filled with the souls of neutral shades who have nowhere else to go, for they are unclaimed by any gods and did nothing in life to suggest any particular ethos. Pilgrims of the philosophy known as the Prophecies of Kalistrade wander these lands, hiding from psychopomps and other creatures while investigating some mysterious agenda within the Spirelands.

**SUBSYSTEMS ELYSIUM \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_**

Category Outer Plane

Divinities Desna, elven pantheon, empyreal lords Native Inhabitants azatas, shades [the chosen)

Verdant, wild, and unrestrained by order, where passion and creation are fostered and rewarded, the plane of Elysium is a place of wild, idealized natural beauty. The so-called Promised Land and its inhabitants represent a wide variety of freely given benevolence, often willing to directly aid visitors but more often serving as inspirations and muses to foster positive change and self-realized success. Elysium's shades, known as the chosen, appear as idealized versions of their mortal selves, each pursuing their own self-determined actions and finding their unique paths to join the ranks of the plane's celestials.

Azatas organize into fleeting, competitive courts, each rewarding heroism and creativity above all else. The Sovyrian elven pantheon and Desna's personal demiplane, Cynosure, reside within Elysium. To be closer to their gods, some elven

planar explorers established the settlement of Refuge as a bastion for creative freedom.

**HEAVEN\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_PLANE**

Category Outer Plane

Divinities Dwarven pantheon, empyreal lords, lomedae Native Inhabitants archons, shades (the elect]\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The great mountain of Heaven is the realm of structured benevolence made manifest. Organized into seven tiers, the mountain's solid appearance is actually malleable, making way for a vast assemblage of varied environments to accommodate both its own celestials and the souls who migrate there. Devoted to defending the innocent and crusading against the wicked, Heavens archons marshal into vast armies, commanded by their empyreal lords. Angels collaborate with the archon legions but typically act in more direct service to Heaven's resident deities, with lomedae the most prominent among them.

Of all the planes. Heaven is perhaps the most skeptical of the technological advancements made by mortals, for knowledge is power, and power corrupts. Resident archons jealously guard and employ technology but leave it out of the hands of Heaven's shades, known as the elect. These shades manifest at the mountain's base, and their subsequent climb up the seemingly endless, unreachable heights is both a literal and figurative journey that they must make without technological assistance.

The settlement of Heaven's Shore is a trader's paradise open to visitors with good intent. However, the area still specializes mostly in magical equipment and only provides the most basic

technological equipment with strict archons guarding advanced gear on higher layers.

**HELL \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_PLANE**

Category Outer Plane

Divinities archdevils, Asmodeus, infernal dukes, queens of the night

Native Inhabitants devils, hell hounds, shades (the damned)

Hell is the realm of devils, the multiversal seat of tyranny and malignant laws, and the divine domain of Asmodeus, the Prince of Darkness. Here, every act is authorized, calculated, recorded, and set like perfectly ordered clockwork within a vastmachine driven on methodical suffering and greased with pain and purification. The nine inverted layers of Hell violently oppose the surrounding fabric of the Maelstrom, each layer shaped to reflect the nature of its ruling archdevil. Avemus's paved volcanic wastes, ruled by Barbatos, are the marshaling place of Hell's armies and hold the enormous construction yards dedicated to crafting fiendish starships. Dispater's layer of Dis is a great iron metropolis of brutal efficiency where goods manufactured in Avernus are distributed. The layer of Erebus is an expansive sewer system for Dis and contains the magitech vaults and treasuries of Hell, ruled by Mammon, a fiend thought to have ascended to a purely digital form along with the evolution of mortal currencies. Ruled by Belial, the layer of Phlegethon hosts Hell's greater forges that equip the vast armies with magical and technological equipment. Geryon's watery realm of Stygia houses Hell's countless data servers and digital libraries. Moloch's smoldering, ash-draped

factories of Malebolge are the training site for the infernal armies, and Baalzebul's frozen layer of Cocytus torments the imprisoned damned in a massive, eternal battle royale. Post-Gap evidence suggests the existence of a supposed tenth layer of Hell, but such references have been mostly scrubbed by the galactic mind wipe.

**MAELSTROM PLANE**

**METAMORPHIC**

**Divinities** Besmara, orc pantheon, protean lords

**Native Inhabitants** proteans, shades [the shapeless) \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The Maelstrom, infinite and ancient spawned the other Outer Planes in cosmological prehistory and surrounds them like a vast metaphorical-and at times literal-ocean of raw, seething quintessence. Where the Maelstrom borders these other planes, its structure takes on their characteristics, albeit in an unpredictable, chaotic fashion. Beyond these so-called Borderlands, however, the Maelstrom reverts to its true nature, the Cerulean Void: a trackless, liquid infinity devoid of stability and permanence where serpentine proteans create and destroy with profound frivolity.The proteans are paradoxically organized into discrete choruses, each with its own philosophy and goals in service to the Maelstrom.

Pre-Drift travel, Besmara crafted the first chaos sa//s that allowed starships to cruise the Maelstrom and achieve fasterthan-light travel. The greatest port of call in the Cerulean Void is Galisemni, an immense metropolis that drifts through the chaos and attracts traders and visitors from across the cosmos.

**NIRVANA PLANE**

**Category** Quter Plane

Divinities Empyreal lords, Sarenrae,Zon-Shelyn **Native Inhabitants** angels, shades [the cleansed)\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Nirvana is the realm of purest benevolence, a pastoral paradise that promises sanctuary to the weary and enlightenment and transcendence to those who seek it out. Filled with beautiful wilderness of all types in perfect harmony with its occupants, Nirvana's wilds are home to angels and others. The plane's shades, known as the cleansed, most often take the forms of glorified, sapient wild animals, though many eventually leave their carefree existence to aid others, ascending to assist the benevolent works of angels. Devoted to guiding and assisting mortals, angels are often charged with delivering important messages that reflect the will of benevolent deities.

While a manifest paradise, Nirvana's luminous perfection has seen a recent change with the addition of a relatively small section of land that's cast in eternal night. This region of rolling hills and forests is lit by stars of unknown origin and is somehow tied to the Netherworld, acting as the realm of Zon-Shelyn.

**OUTER RIHS PLANE**

**Category** Quter Plane

Divinities demon lords, Droskar, Lamashtu, nascent demon lords **Native Inhabitants** demons, shades [larvae) \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The Outer Rifts are realms full of horror and destruction fueled by mortal sin. Each of its innumerable regions is a unique iteration of malice and misery, each with its own terrible and

twisted environment, with one driving maxim: the strong survive, while the weak suffer and are destroyed. Ruled by demons, the Outer Rifts and its native beings seek only to ruin and destroy. Though demons routinely rampage out into other planes when the Outer Rifts manifests a great planar rift, the denizens of the Outer Rifts are fractured and self-destructive. Demon lords such as Pazuzu, Shivaska. and Zura fight for dominion over the Outer Rifts as much or more than they threaten the other planes. **WORLDS Category** Quter Plane

In this modern era, many realms have developed within the Outer Rifts related to the technological advancements of the Universe. The partly digitized Dark Lobby is a brutal realm of corridors and semi-digital fiends managed by the demon lord of games, Icarthian. Vast offices and data storage centers house legions of larvae shades chained to desks and forced to stare at unintelligible monitors, all at the behest of Ksedahl, the demon lord of ceaseless employment.

### DIMENSIONS

Existing in the metaphorical space between the Transitive Planes and smaller, finite demiplanes, dimensions are a category unto themselves, defying the neat categorization of planar scholars and adventurers. Seemingly infinite in scale, not necessarily spatial in the same way as a plane, and overlaying every other plane at once—including one another-dimensions and planes are most significantly differentiated in how each of them breaks the commonly held rules of the other.

# **AKASHIC RECORD DIMENSION PULONIS**

**Category** demiplane

**Divinities** archivists

**Native Inhabitants** Akashic dragons, recorders \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The supernatural repository of all knowledge in the multiverse lies deep within the Astral Plane, completely unreachable except through esoteric techniques known to a few dimensional travelers. An island in a silver sea, this dimension appears to be a grand library, monumental cathedral, or opaline palace, depending on who's looking. The archive's contents appear fluid, simultaneously as vast metal rows of computer servers, stacks of dusty tomes, and constellations of crystals suspended midair.

#### **DIMENSION OF TIME DIMENSION INDEX**

**ERRATIC |**

**Category** dimension

**Divinities** unknown

**Native Inhabitants** shades [the unbound]\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

The Dimension of Time is a swirling storm of blurred images, the composite of millions of interwoven timelines around their own, with a single doorway allowing access to one's own past. Travelers appear as transparent images of themselves, but the plane seems averse to any attempt to alter the past, no matter the means or the intention. Such attempts often end in the offending individual being erased from existence or trapped within closed time loops to limit the damage they inflict upon the overall flow of time.

**GLOSSARY &**

# *.STARFINDER*

Just as there are countless stars throughout the universe, innumerable species coexist on the worlds orbiting them. Also called ancestries, species sometimes appear in multiple star systems or are tethered to just a single world. While many have a humanoid appearance, others have adapted into a variety of different forms and characteristics!

# PAET WORLDS SPECIES

As the core of the Starfinder setting, the species of the Pact Worlds often receive a broad spotlight in the setting. Each of the ancestries included in Starfinder Player Core are a major species within the Pact Worlds, except for skittermanders and vesk who, while critical to the setting, are more often associated with the Veskarium where both species originated. The species described here aren't the only ones living in the Pact Worlds but represent the most common or notable species that might be encountered there.

# Android

Androids are everywhere in the Pact Worlds and throughout the broader cosmos, with different variants existing on hundreds (if not more) worlds. Most androids resemble humans with visible circuitry in their skin or prominent mechanical parts. Creation creches on Aballon and in the Diaspora were known to have manufactured millions of androids during their operation, though several other types of androids have come from outside the Pact Worlds. One notable type is the more ancient android chassis, believed to originate from a hidden world called Androffa.

# Barathu

Barathus are floating, blimp-like creatures from the vast gas giant of Bretheda. They govern Bretheda and several of its surrounding moons as well as the nearby gas giant Liavara. Predisposed to editing their own genetic code and even synthesizing chemicals within their bodies, barathus are capable of innate flight and can adapt to thrive in challenging conditions. A subset of barathus, known as the Dreamers, ceaselessly wander the natural preserves of Liavara. The Dreamers floated in a tranquil dream until the Newborn unleashed a waking nightmare upon them.

### Human

Survivors of their original home world Lost Golarion, humans live on every Pact World and beyond. In bygone eras, some groups of humans established colonies in distant star systems, with none perhaps more notable than the Azlanti Star Empire, a vast interstellar regime founded by the heirs of a human regime that departed Lost Golarion in ages long before the Gap. Humanity's ability to adapt in harsh environments has led to their prominence in the modern age. Some humans still trace their roots back to ethnicities and family lines that existed on Lost Golarion. and their ancestral cultures are commonly honored together by groups such as the Knights of Golarion, who hope to preserve the vanished world's history and traditions.

# Kasatha

Kasathas are a four-armed species from the distant world Kasath. Kasathas have a strong presence in the Pact Worlds thanks to their generational worldship, the /don. The ruling elders known as the Doyenate were originally intent on

settling the world of Akiton, but when the Idari arrived and discovered the planet already inhabited, they stopped their colonization efforts. Generations later, many kasathas have integrated into Pact Worlds society by emigrating onto other planets or staying to help maintain the Idori and its biomes. Some kasathas choose to explore the wider cosmos, often as part of a ritual known as the Tempering, which the Doyenate hope will contribute to the wider mission of discovering a new uniting goal for their species, and perhaps a new destination for their worldship.

# Lashunta

Lashuntas are a dimorphic species known for their telepathy and pheromones that range from pleasant to disturbing, depending on who you ask. Hailing from the world of Castrovel, lashuntas traditionally split themselves into two distinct heritages: damaya and korasha. Damayas are pushed into leadership roles or trained as entertainers or scholars, and they appear delicate and lithe compared to stocky, muscular korashas who are recruited for the military, competitive sports, and roles that involve physical labor. Lashuntas who choose not to become damaya or korasha display unpredictable genetic traits and might appear to be a mix of the two heritages or have more unusual features.

# Pahtra

Pahtras are a felinoid species from Pulonis, the newest planet to join the Pact Worlds alliance after reclaiming their independence from the Veskarium. Pahtras are born with a variety of fur types, colors, and patterns depending on their heritage (or simply at their ancestral god Meyers whim). With their home world as the only recognized Pact World outside of the Pact System, the largest population of pahtras remains physically distant from their Pact Worlds allies, but the long history of occupation by the Veskarium has led to many pahtra families living elsewhere in the galaxy. Some members of the pahtra diaspora have opted to return to Pulonis or have taken to acting as representatives of the newest Pact World on whatever home they'd inhabited before the liberation.

# Shirren

Shirrens<sup>1</sup> ancestors escaped the Swarm, a vicious invading horde of insectile monsters linked by a merciless hive mind. After mutating free will that enabled them to resist the hive mind and break away, they arrived in the Pact Worlds with warning of the Swarm's impending attack. The insectile shirrens' shocking arrival and the vital intelligence they brought with them prevented the Pact Worlds and Veskarium alliance from being defeated by the Swarm. Today, shirrens enjoy their newfound independence by sampling new experiences and personal choices like fine wine. Most shirrens form family groups of three, including a male-female pair and a genderless host who helps with reproduction and child rearing. Shirrens live everywhere, but few stay in one place for long, save for an enclave on Verces and several smaller settlements on other worlds.

# Vsoki

Ysoki families playfully bicker with other clans about their ancestral home world, and even pre-Gap history is muddled on whether the first ysoki originated from Akiton or Lost Golarion. Also referred to as "ratfolk" by some (a term ysoki have adopted), this species can be found everywhere. From single adventurous explorers to entire familial hordes, ysoki eagerly settle down in the nooks and crannies of settlements or on board starships. Cramped environments appeal to their nature, so many ysoki find work as technicians or guides in alien environments. Within the Pact Worlds, ysoki populations are numerous all across Akiton and on board Absalom Station. Ysoki families spread out on every world, leading to the perception that many ysoki have a cousin or auntie in every port of call and backwater station.

# OTHER PACT WORLDS SPECIES

Beyond those presented in P/oyer Core, there are other species present within the Pact Worlds. Some of these species might be more numerous than others, including core ancestries, but might not have as strong a connection with exploration or aren't the focus of as many stories within the setting.

## Ancient Species

All the ancestries found in Pathfinder Pfayer Core still exist within the Pact Worlds in some form. Dwarves maintain a strong presence in the Diaspora where they mine valuable asteroids and dwell in high-tech fortresses. Dwarven clans can also be found in other star systems piloting their vast Star Citadel mobile bases. Most elves are secluded on the continent Sovyrian on their home world of Castrovel where their elders try to maintain a policy of self-isolation that's at odds with the will of younger elves. Gnomes, halflings, and leshies each have small enclaves spread across the Pact Worlds, and many operate independently away from others of their kind. Orcs were a species hit hard by the Gap, and their numbers have become minuscule compared to prior to the event. Many modern orcs believe that most of their ancestors were taken by Torag on whatever fate befell Lost Golarion and that if they prove themselves, they might be elevated to join whatever great conflict their kin were taken to. respiration to survive in space and have adapted to create a set of wings made of ■■Mia

# Pstrazoan

Thought to have originated from inside Apostae, shape-changing astrazoans are a mystery to everyone, including themselves. Most astrazoans hide in plain sight by blending in as mundane members of their community, hiding their true identities and creating new personalities for each "skin," or shape they wear. Rarely, astrazoans flaunt their aberrant true form or even exploit their bizarre talents as assassins, escorts, or spies. Astrazoans mysteriously appeared as a species with no records of their origins and history, and many feel a burning desire to delve the dungeon planet inside Apostae in hopes of uncovering more. Readmore about astrazoans in Storfincfer Go/oxy Guide.

# Contemplatii/i?

Intellectual contemplatives survived in underground sanctuaries on Akiton, where they focused on mental pursuits for so long their bodies atrophied and they became little more than floating brains. Now charged with potent psychic potential, contemplatives tend to dedicate themselves to activities they can enjoy in isolation, like development or research. Adventurous contemplatives might become advisors to less erudite companions. Read more about contemplatives in Starfinder GalaxyGuide.

# Dragonkin

Large-sized draconic humanoids, dragonkin are a species native to Triaxus, the wandering planet. Every dragonkin can form a deep supernatural connection with another person. Partner bonds traditionally form between dragonkin and ryphorians, but any sapient species can enter the bond. Dragonkin and the partner bond are pillars of the legendary Skyfire Legion. Read more about dragonkin in Starfinder Galaxy Guide.

### Kalo

Kalos evolved on the watery moon of Kalo-Mahoi that orbits Bretheda. After breaking free from linnorm oppressors, they built exquisite cities full of art and industry. Kalos infuse art and fashion into everything, even the special environmental protections required for them to survive outside of water. Read more about kalo in Starfinder Ga/axy Guide.

# Sarcesian

Inheritors of the ruined worlds that now form the Diaspora, most sarcesians survive as nomads sailing between creche worlds on solar wings. They can suspend their

pure energy while they're within a vacuum. Sarcesians grow up inside a bubble, then launch into space, where most find adventure, peace, or work in the Pact System's asteroid belt. Read more about sarcesians in Starfinder Go/axy Gurde.

# SRO

Sentient Robotic Organism, or SRO,is a term that refers to almost all types of sentient robots within the Pact Worlds. Independent from semi-organic androids and the soulless anacites of Aballon, SROs represent a wide array of different technological beings created by corporations, scientists, or even artificial gods. SROs all have full rights within the Pact Worlds and have souls that will one day be judged by Pharasma. Read more about SROs in Starfinder TechCore.

# Verthani

The first species to construct spacefaring vessels within the Pact Worlds, the verthanis of Verces adapted technology early in their evolution and incorporated it into their traditional caste system for generations. Spindly-limbed humanoids with pure black eyes, all verthanis can change the pigment of their skin to display complex patterns. Verthanis are the main demographic on the technologically advanced world of Verces. Read more about Verthanis in Storfinder TechCore.

### OTHER EOLOCTIC SPECIES

While the galaxy is full of countless other species, a notable few are found commonly in the Pact Worlds and across the Starfinder setting.

# Skittermander

A four-armed species with colorful fur, skittermanders are perhaps best known for their desire to "help'' whenever they can. Helping is a cultural mandate and evolutionary drive for skittermanders, who evolved on the pastoral world Oeddertchonk. Perhaps no better example exists of this helpful nature than when invaders from the Veskarium came to conquer the skittermander home world, and the skittermanders eagerly assisted their newfound conquerors in the attempt! Today, many skittermanders have migrated to the Pact Worlds (especially onto recently liberated Pulonis), while others still live within the Veskarium. where they handle occupation with a mixture of baffling helpfulness, malicious compliance, and relentless chaos. Regardless of where they live, skittermanders have a reputation for their emotive, helpful natures that might not match every individual's personality.

# Vesk

A warlike, scaly ancestry from the Ghavaniska System, the reptilian species of Vesk Prime are the leading species in the mighty Veskarium empire. Onetime enemies of the Pact Worlds during a tense period of conflict called the Silent War. they put aside their differences to fight the Swarm War. Many vesk forged close ties within the Pact Worlds, and some even left the Veskarium to make lives within the Pact System or other Pact Worlds colonies. Vesk are a divided species, with most still loyally serving the Veskarium in their mounting war with the Azlanti Star Empire, while others distance their lives from affiliation with the Veskarium by pursuing different goals in service to other institutions.

### Vlaka

Vlakas are a bipedal, wolflike ancestry hailing from the distant world of Lajok. far in the Vast. Living on a desolate world orbiting a dying star, vlakas are a reflective species who seek to control their aggressive impulses and to master their emotions in the face of adversity. Known for wildly varying senses, many vlakas have some combination of deafness and/or blindness, while their senses of touch and smell are keenly honed. Adventurous vlakas departed Lajok in search of ways to reverse the death of their home world's star, but those who remained witnessed a miracle. Read more about vlakas in Starfinder Ga/axy Guide.

# *.STARFINDER*

# **THREATS**

The galaxy of Desna'sPath is threatened. Some of these menaces are powerful but selfish entities acting alone, while others are groups or entire civilizations that threaten the galaxy's stability. Larger groups aren't monoliths, and many individuals can be negotiated with or might even have cordial relationships with adventurers. Some might just have an opposing viewpoint for a practical reason, such as necessity demanded by biology or control from an outside force.

The following threats have important roles to play in the Starfinder setting, particularly in relation to the Pact Worlds.

# AZLANTI STAR EMPIRE

Located in the Vast near the region of space known as the Hydra Nebula, the Azlanti Star Empire is an offshoot of humanity who departed Golarion using magical innovation in eras even before the Gap. Settling on the world of New Thespera, the empire expanded massively following the advent of Drift travel by deploying elite Aeon Guard forces in critical locations and establishing colonies across massive swathes of space. Following a brief encounter with a Starfinder Society explorer, the Azlanti Star Empire's ruling Aeon Throne became aware of the Pact System's location but has yet to launch any forays to their ancestral home or any campaigns to find Lost Golarion. Only recently, a sudden and brutal coup instigated by the high priest of Lissala, Ishmerai Ophir, caused a dramatic shift in power within the empire. The reigning Ixomander twins were deposed, ending in one twin's exile and the other's assassination, and the state church of Lissala assumed regency of the empire to prevent further chaos and establish the next heir to the throne. Linder Ishmerai's leadership, the Azlanti Star Empire deployed their new Sihedron Guard fleet to invade Kehtaria, where a fragile peace between Veskarium and Azlanti forces stranded during the Drift Crisis turned into all-out war between the two powers.

# CORPORATIONS

Hundreds of major corporations operate within the Pact Worlds, and even more are active in Near Spaceand the Vast. A few megacorporations like AbadarCorp are powerful enough to have established branches and operations all over the galaxy, but most operate on a smaller scale. Many corporations act as beneficial institutions, while others operate at the fringes of legality. Unethical companies can quickly come into conflict with adventurers or even act as temporary sponsors. Frozen Trove is a Triaxus-based corporation under draconic management, though most of their corporate facilities exist outside the Pact Worlds. Frozen Trove is infamous for performing illegal and unethical experiments in remote locations or even on pre-Drift capable worlds, where they can test their biotech prototypes and study genetically engineered organisms that only exist offthe-record. Broader in scope, azrinaran corporations represent

the generational achievements of elven families who escaped Golarion during the Gap and settled on Apostae. Rampant with nepotism and transactions that skirt Pact Worlds legality, azrinaran groups represent some of the most well-known corporations in the Pact Worlds, including Arabani Arms and Zeizerer Munitions.

# CORPSE FLEET

Following the signing of the Pact and the formation of the Pact Worlds, many powerful undead on Eox disagreed with their leaders' decision to join a federation that would put them on equal footing with the living species of their system. Following the leadership of decorated officer Undying Admiral Shathrava, the Corpse Fleet broke away from Eoxian society and escaped into the Vast. Having spent centuries building their power base, the Corpse Fleet is now an undead civilization unto itself, with strongholds spread throughout the Vast and even into the entropic plane of the Void. Loyal Corpse Fleet privateers believe in the supremacy of undeath over the living, who they believe are useful only as fodder for future undead. Shathrava's goal is unifying all undead privateer fleets into a force large enough to bring the Pact Worlds' fleets to heel and establish undead supremacy within the Pact System by seizing the Starstone.

# CULT OF THE DEVOURER

A force of nature as much as a religion, the servants of the Devourer act impulsively to spread chaos and destruction across the galaxy. People who pledge to serve the god of cosmic annihilation are often sadistic cannibals and berserkers who dedicate themselves to honoring their patron through acts of mass destruction and violence. Such groups rarely maintain cohesion. Leaders quickly emerge to enact a bloody agenda before burning themselves out [sometimes literally]. The only consistent threat from the Devourer's unstable cult are atrocites-monsters who escaped Pharasma's judgment to continually serve the Devourer as agents of oblivion. Atrocites are often summoned by cultists desiring maximum carnage.

## FIRST ONES

This mysterious precursor species established outposts on a handful of worlds and then disappeared without any indication of where they went. The forlorn remnants of their

civilization remain, including their abandoned progeny, the robotic anacites of Aballon. In their creators' absence, many anacites strive to become modern incarnations of the First Ones or seek reconnection with their lost progenitors. Recent discoveries by the Starfinder Society and other organizations have hinted at a wider First Ones presence throughout the cosmos, including discoveries of ruins within the Pact System's sun as well as orbiting the sun of the vlaka home world of Lajok. The First Ones returned to the modern galactic stage with the sudden arrival of a mysterious starship into the Pact System followed by its partially controlled crash onto the surface of Akiton. Known as the Wreck of the Returned, this vessel bears many similarities to the mysterious technology of the First Ones and has unleashed defenses well beyond the Pact Worlds' understanding of magic and technology. Was the vessel a gift, or a weapon?

## GIDERON OUTHORIT'r'

Sometime during the Gap, it's believed that a great civilization existed in Near Space, founded by goblinoid colonists who left the Pact System long ago. Following the Gap. this empire had fallen and was replaced by two major powers: the Marixah Republic and the Gideron Authority. Whereas the Marixah Republic is a democratic civilization with strong ties to the Pact Worlds, the Gideron Authority is an authoritarian military regime with delusions of reclaiming the glory of the empire both sides have no recollection of. Though smaller in size compared to the relative giants of the Azlanti Empire and the Veskarium, the Gideron Authority and its mighty platoons of goblin troops have their eye on surrounding territories and don't seem likely to stop expanding anytime soon.

# INSATIABLE ECLIPSE

The birth of the Newborn in the Pact Worlds called forth a terror from Eox's distant past. The remorseless undead entity known as the Insatiable Eclipse has since begun to reclaim its own domain on the grave world. Eox's ruling bone sages have done their best to silence news of the harrowing destruction of Murthal, the ancient necropolis above the Veiled Tomb. The Insatiable Eclipse won't be sated until it drinks the life

force of all living creatures. Its crazed followers seek the peace they believe will follow the extinction of all life.

# JINSUL HIEROCRACM

The chitinous species known as jinsuls were first encountered by Pact Worlders when the Starfinder Society began exploring the Scoured Stars. Jinsuls believe that only the descendants of the eight j civilizations originally from the Scoured Stars are worthy to exist in the universe. They believe j® the waking deity Kadrical created them for the purpose of shepherding his flock into a new age and purging the wolves. Starfinder Society agents defeated Kadrical's herald \*\* Sivarm Bastorox

in a climactic showdown that broke the jinsuls' resolve and scattered them throughout the Vast.Now led by errant hierarchs, jinsul fleets prey on trade lanes and attack settlements in Near Space and the Vast, following an inscrutable agenda. Hierarch Jurastroon commands the largest contingent of jinsul warriors from their mobile fortress world of Otorak.

# SWARM

With numbers beyond measure, the monstrous, insectile Swarm is perhaps the greatest threat to the stability of the galaxy thus far discovered. United by a ferocious hive mind, the Swarm devours worlds and adapts. The threat of just even a single colony brought the Pact Worlds and the Veskarium together as a unified force, though the colony's defeat came at a high cost. Since a colony was defeated in the Swarm War, the greater Swarm has avoided Near Space and begun eating a path through the Vast.

The relatively recent discovery of Swarm life-forms on Krethiskar within Near Space let loose a previously dormant colony. The Krethiskar colony has since expanded rapidly into Near Space, attacking the worlds of the Szandite Collective without mercy. The Krethiskar Swarm is rumored to be commanded by a mutated shirren with a familiar face. Krethiskar Swarm components have a different appearance and composition than those of the true Swarm, but this deviant lineage has thus far proven just as tenacious as the limitless hordes of Swarm lurking in the Vast.
